forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+title: Chat copy must populate the vim register so CM6 `p` pastes it
+---
+## What
+
+In the AI chat, the per-message **Copy** button (and the code-block copy buttons inside tool folds) only writes the OS clipboard via `navigator.clipboard.writeText`. So after copying, pressing **`p`** in a CM6 vim editor (the composer, or any field editor) pastes nothing — the copied text never lands where vim's `p` reads.
+
+### Root cause (researched)
+
+- `MessageActionBar.handleCopy` in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.tsx:662-674` does `navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)`. The code-block copy in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-elements/code-block.tsx:156` does the same.
+- The app's vim mode is `@replit/codemirror-vim` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-keymap.ts` → `vim()`), no clipboard config.
+- In `@replit/codemirror-vim`, plain `p` pastes **synchronously** from the register named by `actionArgs.registerName` — `undefined` for a bare `p`, which resolves to the **unnamed register** (`"`). Only `"+p` (`registerName === '+'`) does `navigator.clipboard.readText()`. (See the `paste:` action in the vendored `dist/index.js`.) The unnamed register is populated by in-editor yanks/deletes, never by an external `writeText`. Hence bare `p` sees an empty/stale register.
+- The vim register controller is module-global (`vimGlobalState`) and exposed via `Vim.getRegisterController()` (typed in `dist/index.d.ts`): `getRegisterController().getRegister(name).setText(text, linewise?, blockwise?)` and `.unnamedRegister.setText(...)`.
+
+### Approach
+
+1. Add a shared helper `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/clipboard.ts` — `copyText(text: string): Promise<void>` that:
+   - `await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)` (OS clipboard — keeps CUA/emacs Cmd/Ctrl+V and system paste working), and
+   - mirrors the text into the vim registers so a bare `p` pastes it: `Vim.getRegisterController()` then set the unnamed register `"` and the yank register `0` (and `+` for `"+p`/clipboard consistency) via `setText(text)`. Wrap the vim-register part in a `try/catch` (best-effort): if vim global state isn't initialized yet — e.g. no vim editor has mounted — silently skip; the OS clipboard write already happened. Import `Vim` from `@replit/codemirror-vim` (already used in `components/fields/text-editor.tsx`).
+   - Keep the existing error handling shape (log on `writeText` failure).
+2. Use `copyText` in both copy sites: `ai-panel.tsx` `MessageActionBar.handleCopy` and `ai-elements/code-block.tsx` (note: `code-block.tsx` is a vendored AI-Elements file — make a minimal swap of the `writeText` call for `copyText`, matching its existing style; do not reformat the file).
+
+### Non-goals
+
+- Do not change vim keybindings or how `p` works; do not try to make plain `p` async-read the OS clipboard (codemirror-vim's non-`+` paste path is synchronous and cannot await `readText`).
+- Do not touch CUA/emacs paste — `writeText` already covers them; this only *adds* the vim-register mirror.
+- Do not change unrelated clipboard usages outside the chat.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] After `copyText(text)` runs, a CM6 editor in vim mode pastes `text` on a bare `p` (normal mode) — not just on `"+p`.
+- [ ] `copyText` still writes the OS clipboard (CUA/emacs paste and system paste unaffected).
+- [ ] Both the per-message Copy button and the tool-fold code-block Copy button route through `copyText`.
+- [ ] When the vim register controller is unavailable, `copyText` still writes the OS clipboard and does not throw.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Unit test `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/clipboard.test.ts`: with `navigator.clipboard.writeText` and `Vim.getRegisterController` stubbed/observed, `copyText("hi")` calls `writeText("hi")` and sets the unnamed (`"`) and `0` registers to `"hi"`; and when `getRegisterController` throws, `writeText` still ran and no error propagates.
+- [ ] Behavioral browser test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx` or a sibling): mount a vim-mode CM6 editor, call `copyText("PASTE_ME")`, focus the editor in normal mode, press `p`, and assert the buffer contains `PASTE_ME`. Must FAIL before the fix (bare `p` pastes nothing from an external copy) and PASS after.
+- [ ] `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test clipboard` and `... test ai-prompt-composer` green.
+- [ ] Full UI suite green: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test`.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "bare `p` pastes externally-copied text" test first, then add `copyText` (writeText + vim-register mirror) and route the copy buttons through it.
+
+#bug
\ No newline at end of file

-In the AI chat, the per-message **Copy** button (and the code-block copy buttons inside tool folds) only writes the OS clipboard via `navigator.clipboard.writeText`. -
-### Root cause (researched)

-- `MessageActionBar.handleCopy` in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.tsx:662-674` does `navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)`. The code-block copy in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-elements/code-block.tsx:156` does the same.

-- In `@replit/codemirror-vim`, plain `p` pastes **synchronously** from the register named by `actionArgs.registerName` — `undefined` for a bare `p`, which resolves to the **unnamed register** (`"`). Only `"+p` (`registerName === '+'`) does `navigator.clipboard.readText()`. (See the `paste:` action in the vendored `dist/index.js`.) The unnamed register is populated by in-editor yanks/deletes, never by an external `writeText`. Hence bare `p` sees an empty/stale register.
-- The vim register controller is module-global (`vimGlobalState`) and exposed via `Vim.getRegisterController()` (typed in `dist/index.d.ts`): `getRegisterController().getRegister(name).setText(text, linewise?, blockwise?)` and `.unnamedRegister.setText(...)`.
-
-### Approach
-
-1. Add a shared helper `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/clipboard.ts` — `copyText(text: string): Promise<void>` that:
-   - `await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)` (OS clipboard — keeps CUA/emacs Cmd/Ctrl+V and system paste working), and
-   - mirrors the text into the vim registers so a bare `p` pastes it: `Vim.getRegisterController()` then set the unnamed register `"` and the yank register `0` (and `+` for `"+p`/clipboard consistency) via `setText(text)`. Wrap the vim-register part in a `try/catch` (best-effort): if vim global state isn't initialized yet — e.g. no vim editor has mounted — silently skip; the OS clipboard write already happened. Import `Vim` from `@replit/codemirror-vim` (already used in `components/fields/text-editor.tsx`).
-   - Keep the existing error handling shape (log on `writeText` failure).
-2. Use `copyText` in both copy sites: `ai-panel.tsx` `MessageActionBar.handleCopy` and `ai-elements/code-block.tsx` (note: `code-block.tsx` is a vendored AI-Elements file — make a minimal swap of the `writeText` call for `copyText`, matching its existing style; do not reformat the file).
-
-### Non-goals
-
-- Do not change vim keybindings or how `p` works; do not try to make plain `p` async-read the OS clipboard (codemirror-vim's non-`+` paste path is synchronous and cannot await `readText`).
-- Do not touch CUA/emacs paste — `writeText` already covers them; this only *adds* the vim-register mirror.
-- Do not change unrelated clipboard usages outside the chat.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-
-- [ ] After `copyText(text)` runs, a CM6 editor in vim mode pastes `text` on a bare `p` (normal mode) — not just on `"+p`.
-- [ ] `copyText` still writes the OS clipboard (CUA/emacs paste and system paste unaffected).
-- [ ] Both the per-message Copy button and the tool-fold code-block Copy button route through `copyText`.
-- [ ] When the vim register controller is unavailable, `copyText` still writes the OS clipboard and does not throw.
-
-## Tests
-
-- [ ] Unit test `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/clipboard.test.ts`: with `navigator.clipboard.writeText` and `Vim.getRegisterController` stubbed/observed, `copyText("hi")` calls `writeText("hi")` and sets the unnamed (`"`) and `0` registers to `"hi"`; and when `getRegisterController` throws, `writeText` still ran and no error propagates.
-- [ ] Behavioral browser test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx` or a sibling): mount a vim-mode CM6 editor, call `copyText("PASTE_ME")`, focus the editor in normal mode, press `p`, and assert the buffer contains `PASTE_ME`. Must FAIL before the fix (bare `p` pastes nothing from an external copy) and PASS after.
-- [ ] `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test clipboard` and `... test ai-prompt-composer` green.
-- [ ] Full UI suite green: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test`.
-
-## Workflow
-
-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "bare `p` pastes externally-copied text" test first, then add `copyText` (writeText + vim-register mirror) and route the copy buttons through it.
-

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -32,20 +32,24 @@

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] After `copyText(text)` runs, a CM6 editor in vim mode pastes `text` on a bare `p` (normal mode) — not just on `"+p`.
-- [ ] `copyText` still writes the OS clipboard (CUA/emacs paste and system paste unaffected).
-- [ ] Both the per-message Copy button and the tool-fold code-block Copy button route through `copyText`.
-- [ ] When the vim register controller is unavailable, `copyText` still writes the OS clipboard and does not throw.
+- [x] After `copyText(text)` runs, a CM6 editor in vim mode pastes `text` on a bare `p` (normal mode) — not just on `"+p`.
+- [x] `copyText` still writes the OS clipboard (CUA/emacs paste and system paste unaffected).
+- [x] Both the per-message Copy button and the tool-fold code-block Copy button route through `copyText`.
+- [x] When the vim register controller is unavailable, `copyText` still writes the OS clipboard and does not throw.

 ## Tests

-- [ ] Unit test `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/clipboard.test.ts`: with `navigator.clipboard.writeText` and `Vim.getRegisterController` stubbed/observed, `copyText("hi")` calls `writeText("hi")` and sets the unnamed (`"`) and `0` registers to `"hi"`; and when `getRegisterController` throws, `writeText` still ran and no error propagates.
-- [ ] Behavioral browser test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx` or a sibling): mount a vim-mode CM6 editor, call `copyText("PASTE_ME")`, focus the editor in normal mode, press `p`, and assert the buffer contains `PASTE_ME`. Must FAIL before the fix (bare `p` pastes nothing from an external copy) and PASS after.
-- [ ] `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test clipboard` and `... test ai-prompt-composer` green.
-- [ ] Full UI suite green: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test`.
+- [x] Unit test `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/clipboard.test.ts`: with `navigator.clipboard.writeText` and `Vim.getRegisterController` stubbed/observed, `copyText("hi")` calls `writeText("hi")` and sets the unnamed (`"`) and `0` registers to `"hi"`; and when `getRegisterController` throws, `writeText` still ran and no error propagates.
+- [x] Behavioral browser test (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx` or a sibling): mount a vim-mode CM6 editor, call `copyText("PASTE_ME")`, focus the editor in normal mode, press `p`, and assert the buffer contains `PASTE_ME`. Must FAIL before the fix (bare `p` pastes nothing from an external copy) and PASS after.
+- [x] `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test clipboard` and `... test ai-prompt-composer` green.
+- [x] Full UI suite green: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test`.

 ## Workflow

 - Use `/tdd` — write the failing "bare `p` pastes externally-copied text" test first, then add `copyText` (writeText + vim-register mirror) and route the copy buttons through it.

+### Implementation note
+
+The behavioral browser test runs in headless Chromium, which denies the `clipboard-write` permission, so the real `navigator.clipboard.writeText` cannot be exercised there (the real `writeText` call is pinned by the unit test with a spy instead). The behavioral test therefore spies on `writeText` (stub it to resolve) and drives the *other* half of `copyText` — the vim-register mirror — by a real bare-`p` keystroke that must paste `PASTE_ME`. It still genuinely fails before the fix (no mirror → `p` pastes nothing) and passes after.
+
 #bug
\ No newline at end of file

+### Approach (done)

-   - mirrors the text into the vim registers so a bare `p` pastes it: `Vim.getRegisterController()` then set the unnamed register `"` and the yank register `0` (and `+` for `"+p`/clipboard consistency) via `setText(text)`. +1. New `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/clipboard.ts` — `copyText(text)` mirrors the text into the vim registers (unnamed `"`, `0`, `+`) via `Vim.getRegisterController()` (best-effort, try/catch) **and** `await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)`.
+2. Routed both copy sites through `copyText`: `ai-panel.tsx` `MessageActionBar.handleCopy` and the vendored `ai-elements/code-block.tsx` (minimal swap, no reformat).

+### Implementation note
+
+The behavioral browser test runs in headless Chromium, which denies `clipboard-write`, so the real `writeText` is pinned by the unit test (spy) while the behavioral test drives the vim-register mirror with a real bare-`p` keystroke. It fails before the fix (no mirror → `p` pastes nothing) and passes after.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-24 17:30 — task-mode, reviewer subagent)

-## Workflow
+0 blockers, 1 warning, 1 nit — both resolved.

-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "bare `p` pastes externally-copied text" test first, then add `copyText` (writeText + vim-register mirror) and route the copy buttons through it.
+### Warnings
+- [x] `clipboard.ts` `copyText` ordering — the mirror was gated on `writeText` success (`await writeText` first), so a denied OS write (restricted clipboard permission) would skip the vim mirror and leave bare `p` empty — gating the robust feature on the fragile one. Fixed: `copyText` now mirrors to the vim registers FIRST, then `await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)` (write error still re-thrown for caller logging). So `p` works even when the OS write is refused.

-### Implementation note
+### Nits
+- [x] `clipboard.test.ts` `makeRegister` — abbreviated `setText(t?)` param renamed to `text` per the JS/TS no-abbreviations guideline.

-The behavioral browser test runs in headless Chromium, which denies the `clipboard-write` permission, so the real `navigator.clipboard.writeText` cannot be exercised there (the real `writeText` call is pinned by the unit test with a spy instead). The behavioral test therefore spies on `writeText` (stub it to resolve) and drives the *other* half of `copyText` — the vim-register mirror — by a real bare-`p` keystroke that must paste `PASTE_ME`. It still genuinely fails before the fix (no mirror → `p` pastes nothing) and passes after.
+Re-verified after fixes: full UI suite green (257 files, 2443 tests; tsc clean).

 ### Root cause (researched)

-- `MessageActionBar.handleCopy` in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.tsx` did `navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)`. The code-block copy in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-elements/code-block.tsx` did the same.
-- The app's vim mode is `@replit/codemirror-vim` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-keymap.ts` → `vim()`).
-- In `@replit/codemirror-vim`, plain `p` pastes **synchronously** from the **unnamed register** (`"`); only `"+p` reads the OS clipboard. The unnamed register is populated by in-editor yanks/deletes, never by an external `writeText`. Hence bare `p` saw an empty/stale register.
-- The vim register controller is module-global and exposed via `Vim.getRegisterController()`.
+- `MessageActionBar.handleCopy` in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.tsx:662-674` does `navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)`. The code-block copy in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-elements/code-block.tsx:156` does the same.
+- The app's vim mode is `@replit/codemirror-vim` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-keymap.ts` → `vim()`), no clipboard config.
+- In `@replit/codemirror-vim`, plain `p` pastes **synchronously** from the register named by `actionArgs.registerName` — `undefined` for a bare `p`, which resolves to the **unnamed register** (`"`). Only `"+p` (`registerName === '+'`) does `navigator.clipboard.readText()`. (See the `paste:` action in the vendored `dist/index.js`.) The unnamed register is populated by in-editor yanks/deletes, never by an external `writeText`. Hence bare `p` sees an empty/stale register.
+- The vim register controller is module-global (`vimGlobalState`) and exposed via `Vim.getRegisterController()` (typed in `dist/index.d.ts`): `getRegisterController().getRegister(name).setText(text, linewise?, blockwise?)` and `.unnamedRegister.setText(...)`.

-### Approach (done)
+### Approach

-1. New `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/clipboard.ts` — `copyText(text)` mirrors the text into the vim registers (unnamed `"`, `0`, `+`) via `Vim.getRegisterController()` (best-effort, try/catch) **and** `await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)`.
-2. Routed both copy sites through `copyText`: `ai-panel.tsx` `MessageActionBar.handleCopy` and the vendored `ai-elements/code-block.tsx` (minimal swap, no reformat).
+1. Add a shared helper `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/clipboard.ts` — `copyText(text: string): Promise<void>` that:
+   - `await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)` (OS clipboard — keeps CUA/emacs Cmd/Ctrl+V and system paste working), and
+   - mirrors the text into the vim registers so a bare `p` pastes it: `Vim.getRegisterController()` then set the unnamed register `"` and the yank register `0` (and `+` for `"+p`/clipboard consistency) via `setText(text)`. Wrap the vim-register part in a `try/catch` (best-effort): if vim global state isn't initialized yet — e.g. no vim editor has mounted — silently skip; the OS clipboard write already happened. Import `Vim` from `@replit/codemirror-vim` (already used in `components/fields/text-editor.tsx`).
+   - Keep the existing error handling shape (log on `writeText` failure).
+2. Use `copyText` in both copy sites: `ai-panel.tsx` `MessageActionBar.handleCopy` and `ai-elements/code-block.tsx` (note: `code-block.tsx` is a vendored AI-Elements file — make a minimal swap of the `writeText` call for `copyText`, matching its existing style; do not reformat the file).

 ### Non-goals

-- No vim keybinding changes; no attempt to make bare `p` async-read the OS clipboard (codemirror-vim's non-`+` paste is synchronous).
-- CUA/emacs paste unaffected.
+- Do not change vim keybindings or how `p` works; do not try to make plain `p` async-read the OS clipboard (codemirror-vim's non-`+` paste path is synchronous and cannot await `readText`).
+- Do not touch CUA/emacs paste — `writeText` already covers them; this only *adds* the vim-register mirror.
+- Do not change unrelated clipboard usages outside the chat.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [x] Behavioral browser test in `ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx`: vim-mode CM6 editor, `copyText("PASTE_ME")`, real bare `p`, asserts the buffer contains `PASTE_ME`. Fail-before / pass-after.
+- [x] Unit test `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/clipboard.test.ts`: with `navigator.clipboard.writeText` and `Vim.getRegisterController` stubbed/observed, `copyText("hi")` calls `writeText("hi")` and sets the unnamed (`"`) and `0` registers to `"hi"`; and when `getRegisterController` throws, `writeText` still ran and no error propagates.

Must FAIL before the fix (bare `p` pastes nothing from an external copy) and PASS after.

-### Implementation note
+## Workflow

-The behavioral browser test runs in headless Chromium, which denies `clipboard-write`, so the real `writeText` is pinned by the unit test (spy) while the behavioral test drives the vim-register mirror with a real bare-`p` keystroke. It fails before the fix (no mirror → `p` pastes nothing) and passes after.
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "bare `p` pastes externally-copied text" test first, then add `copyText` (writeText + vim-register mirror) and route the copy buttons through it.

-## Review Findings (2026-05-24 17:30 — task-mode, reviewer subagent)
-
-0 blockers, 1 warning, 1 nit — both resolved.
-
-### Warnings
-- [x] `clipboard.ts` `copyText` ordering — the mirror was gated on `writeText` success (`await writeText` first), so a denied OS write (restricted clipboard permission) would skip the vim mirror and leave bare `p` empty — gating the robust feature on the fragile one. Fixed: `copyText` now mirrors to the vim registers FIRST, then `await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)` (write error still re-thrown for caller logging). So `p` works even when the OS write is refused.
-
-### Nits
-- [x] `clipboard.test.ts` `makeRegister` — abbreviated `setText(t?)` param renamed to `text` per the JS/TS no-abbreviations guideline.
+### Implementation note

-Re-verified after fixes: full UI suite green (257 files, 2443 tests; tsc clean).
+The behavioral browser test runs in headless Chromium, which denies the `clipboard-write` permission, so the real `navigator.clipboard.writeText` cannot be exercised there (the real `writeText` call is pinned by the unit test with a spy instead). The behavioral test therefore spies on `writeText` (stub it to resolve) and drives the *other* half of `copyText` — the vim-register mirror — by a real bare-`p` keystroke that must paste `PASTE_ME`. It still genuinely fails before the fix (no mirror → `p` pastes nothing) and passes after.
